skill.name: vercel-automation
skill.description: Automate Vercel tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage deployments, domains, DNS, env vars, projects, and teams. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Vercel platform operations through Composio's Vercel toolkit via Rube MCP for deployment management, domain configuration, and environment variables.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 80 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (Markdown file) describing MCP tool usage for Vercel. Detected 'backtick execution' patterns are code block formatting in Markdown, not shell commands. Network URL is documentation for MCP endpoint. No executable code or security risks present.

medium_findings.title: External Commands - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns. These are Markdown code block delimiters in documentation, not actual command execution. The skill contains no executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: Network URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL https://rube.app/mcp is documentation for MCP server endpoint configuration, not actual network access in code. This is instructional text for users.

low_findings.title: Blocker Patterns - False Positive
low_findings.description: Weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns detected in Markdown text. These are documentation content, not actual cryptographic or reconnaissance code.

content.value_statement: Streamline Vercel platform management through AI-powered automation. This skill enables seamless deployment management, domain configuration, and environment variable control via Composio MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: List and inspect Vercel deployments with filtering by state and target environment
Create new deployments using git sources or file uploads
Manage environment variables across production, preview, and development environments
Configure custom domains and manage DNS records including A, CNAME, MX, and TXT types
View deployment logs, events, and build/runtime error details
Manage team memberships and project settings
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP server connection and Vercel OAuth authentication
Cannot deploy without proper git repository integration or file source
Environment variable changes require new deployments to take effect
Rate limits apply to Vercel API endpoints

use_cases.title: Development Team Lead
use_cases.description: Automate deployment monitoring and debugging across multiple projects. Quickly identify failed builds, inspect logs, and trigger redeployments without leaving the AI chat interface.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads managing multiple Vercel projects

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Streamline environment variable management and DNS configuration. Bulk update environment settings across preview and production environments with automated validation.

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer
use_cases.description: Deploy code changes directly from chat by triggering deployments, monitoring build progress, and verifying production rollouts with real-time status updates.
use_cases.target_user: Developers deploying personal or client projects

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Deployments
prompt_templates.prompt: List the last 10 deployments for my project and show me which ones are in production.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Failed Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: My deployment failed with an ERROR state. Get the deployment logs and events to identify what went wrong.

prompt_templates.title: Add Environment Variables
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new environment variable DATABASE_URL to my project for production and preview environments.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Custom Domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the DNS configuration for example.com and create the necessary DNS records to connect it to my Vercel project.

output_examples.output: Found 5 deployments for my-nextjs-app: 2 READY (1 production, 1 preview), 1 BUILDING, 2 ERROR. Production deployment dpl_abc123 was deployed 2 hours ago.
Build logs show successful Next.js compilation. Runtime logs indicate serverless function timeout after 10s on /api/heavy-endpoint.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Vercel operations
Use deployment events and logs to debug timing issues and build failures
anti_patterns: Do not poll the API excessively when monitoring deployments - use appropriate polling intervals
Do not provide both gitSource and files parameters when creating deployments - choose one
Do not expect to read back secret environment variable values after creation - they are write-only

faq.question: What do I need to set up before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP connected (https://rube.app/mcp) and an active Vercel connection via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the vercel toolkit. Follow the OAuth flow to authenticate.

faq.question: Can I deploy to both preview and production environments?
faq.answer: Yes, specify the target parameter as 'preview' or 'production' when creating deployments. Production deployments update the production domain alias automatically.

faq.question: Why can't I read back my secret environment variable?
faq.answer: Environment variables with type 'secret' are write-only for security. Only the ID is returned after creation. Use type 'plain' for variables you need to read.

faq.question: How do I find my project ID?
faq.answer: Use VERCEL_LIST_PROJECTS to list all projects, then find your project by name and extract the id field for subsequent operations.

faq.question: What deployment states should I monitor?
faq.answer: Deployments progress through: INITIALIZING, ANALYZING, BUILDING, DEPLOYING, and finally READY or ERROR. Only READY deployments are live and serving traffic.

faq.question: Can I manage DNS records for any domain?
faq.answer: The domain must first be added to your Vercel account. DNS management is only available for domains you own through Vercel.
